name: DOM-Based XSS
educational_objective: Understand that DOM XSS originates entirely in the browser — the server never sees the payload. The vulnerability is in client-side JavaScript that reads from window.location and writes to innerHTML without sanitisation.
scenario: PortalDash v1.8 — an internal dashboard. The page reads ?msg= from the URL on load and writes it via document.getElementById('notification').innerHTML = msg. No server-side processing occurs for this parameter.
ar_scenario: لوحة PortalDash تقرأ ?msg= من URL وتكتبه مباشرةً في innerHTML دون أي معالجة من السيرفر.

hints_metadata.content: The ?msg= parameter is read by JavaScript in the browser, not by the server. Look at how the notification element is populated.
hints_metadata.ar_content: معامل ?msg= يُقرأ بواسطة JavaScript في المتصفح وليس السيرفر. انظر كيف يُملأ عنصر الإشعار.

hints_metadata.content: The msg value is written into innerHTML directly. Any HTML you inject becomes live DOM. Try a tag with an event handler that fires without user interaction.
hints_metadata.ar_content: قيمة msg تُكتب في innerHTML مباشرةً. أي HTML تحقنه يصبح DOM حياً.

hints_metadata.content: Craft the URL: ?msg= — paste it in the browser bar and send. The DOM sink fires immediately on page render.
hints_metadata.ar_content: اصنع الرابط: ?msg= وألصقه في شريط المتصفح.

flag_validation.note: Validated via /verify endpoint — student submits crafted URL, server confirms payload presence

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: GET /dashboard?msg=<img+src%3Dx+onerror%3Dalert(1)> → JS reads msg → innerHTML write → onerror fires → POST /verify confirms
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Use textContent instead of innerHTML; sanitise URL params with DOMPurify before any DOM write; enforce CSP
